codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly identifies and removes the duplicate output_content.append(text) call at line 316 in kiro/routes/openai.py. The fix is minimal, surgical, and addresses the exact issue described in the feature requirements. The duplicate was causing Anthropic streaming content to be counted twice in token calculations. After the fix, content is appended only once per chunk (line 314), and all 205 existing tests pass without regression.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase has multiple similar content collection patterns across different streaming formats (OpenAI SSE at line 303, Anthropic SSE at line 314, and another Anthropic section at lines 567-573). While the duplicate was fixed in one location, there's no documented convention in AGENTS.md about the pattern for collecting streaming content to prevent similar issues in the future.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: kiro/routes/openai.py has 4 output_content.append() calls across 3 different streaming format handlers. The duplicate at line 316 was likely introduced during copy-paste refactoring. AGENTS.md doesn't mention streaming content collection patterns or common pitfalls.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill was followed correctly. The worker used TDD approach (verified tests pass), made minimal changes, and provided proper verification. The skill procedure worked well for this simple bug fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker followed procedure: read context, identified issue with grep, made surgical edit, ran pytest (205 tests passed), committed with proper message format. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations.

summary: Feature m2-f7-fix-duplicate-content successfully removes the duplicate output_content.append(text) line that was causing thinking content to be counted twice. The fix is a single-line deletion at line 316, removing the redundant append call while keeping the correct one at line 314. All 205 tests pass, confirming no regressions. The implementation fully satisfies the feature description and fulfills assertion x-a4 (error handling preserves system stability). Code review passes with no blocking issues. One minor observation: the codebase would benefit from documented conventions around streaming content collection patterns to prevent similar copy-paste errors in the future.
